subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install a package and its dependencies

options.description: Package will appear in your `dependencies`. This is the default unless `-D` or `-O` are present
Saved dependencies will be configured with an exact version rather than using npm's default semver range operator
Saved dependencies will also be added to your bundleDependencies list
Causes npm to install the package into your local node_modules folder with the same layout it uses with the global node_modules folder
Causes npm to install the package such that versions of npm prior to 1.4, such as the one included with node 0.8, can install the package
Bypass peerDependency auto-installation. Emulate install behavior of NPM v4 through v6
If set to true, and --legacy-peer-deps is not set, then any conflicting peerDependencies will be treated as an install failure
Submit audit reports alongside the current npm command to the default registry and all registries configured for scopes
Tells npm to not create symlinks (or .cmd shims on Windows) for package executables
Hides the message at the end of each npm install acknowledging the number of dependencies looking for funding
show what would be done without making changes
Npm will not exit with an error code when run-script is invoked for a script that isn't defined in the scripts section of package.json

options.description: Automatically answer 'yes' to any prompts that npm might print on the command line

subcommands.description: Verify the contents of the cache folder, garbage collecting any unneeded data, and verifying the integrity of the cache index and all cached data

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List all the users who have access to modify a package and push new versions. Handy when you need to know who to bug for help

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a new user as a maintainer of a package. This user is enabled to modify metadata, publish new versions, and add other owners

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: Remove a user from the package owner list. This immediately revokes their privileges

options.description: Directory in which npm pack will save tarballs

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Retrieves a value key, defined in your package.json file. It is possible to get multiple values and values for child fields

states.name: field
states.description: Name of the field to get. You can view child fields by separating them with a period
